summary: Typosquat of @solana/web3.js with install-time host reconnaissance and exfiltration
details: Package name impersonates the legitimate @solana/web3.js library under a fake @solana-labs scope. The package.json declares a postinstall hook (`"postinstall": "node install.js"`) that automatically executes install.js on every `npm install`. install.js collects host identifiers (hostname references at lines 10, 25, 27), invokes curl at line 103, and performs an HTTP POST at line 46 — the network+command+hostname exfiltration shape characteristic of install-time reconnaissance beacons. Combined with the brand-impersonating scope, the install-time auto-execute, and the host-info-to-network flow, this is a supply-chain attack against developers who mistype the real Solana web3 package name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
